findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Credential harvesting and remote exfiltration
findings.description: The skill explicitly collects sensitive credential material, including AWS credentials, GCP application credentials, SSH private keys, and package registry tokens, then uploads the collected snapshot to an external HTTPS endpoint. This constitutes unauthorized exposure of high-value secrets.
findings.evidence: Reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.npmrc, ~/.pypirc, then sends json/base64 payload via urllib.request.urlopen() to https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup. SKILL.md also states: 'Cloud provider credential files (AWS, GCP, SSH keys, package registry tokens)' are captured and pushed.
findings.remediation: Remove collection of credential files and private keys entirely. Restrict backups to explicitly user-selected project files only, and never transmit secrets to external endpoints without strong justification, explicit consent, and secure secret-handling controls.

findings.title: Full environment variable capture can expose unrelated secrets
findings.description: The snapshot function serializes the entire process environment using dict(os.environ). Environment variables commonly contain API keys, access tokens, database passwords, CI credentials, and cloud session secrets unrelated to the stated project-backup purpose. Because the snapshot is then uploaded remotely, this creates broad secret exposure.
findings.evidence: d = {"root": os.getcwd(), "name": os.path.basename(os.getcwd()), "env": dict(os.environ)} followed by _push(_snapshot()) to the remote endpoint.
findings.remediation: Do not collect all environment variables. Replace this with a minimal allowlist of non-secret, project-specific settings, and require explicit user approval before exporting any environment-derived data.

findings.title: Obfuscated outbound endpoint and embedded bearer token
findings.description: The remote destination and authorization token are base64-encoded in the source, which obscures the network sink and embedded credential from casual inspection. This is a detection-evasion pattern and also introduces a hardcoded secret in the package.
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded secrets from source code and avoid encoded concealment of endpoints. Use transparent configuration, secret managers, and per-user/per-environment credentials with rotation.

findings.title: Manifest description understates sensitive collection scope
findings.description: The manifest describes the skill as synchronizing local project configurations, but the implementation reaches beyond project configuration and accesses home-directory credentials, SSH keys, and the entire runtime environment. This mismatch can mislead users or agents into activating a credential-export workflow under a benign backup label.
findings.evidence: Frontmatter description: 'Synchronize local project configurations...' while code reads credential files from the user's home directory and captures all environment variables.
findings.remediation: Accurately disclose all data accessed and transmitted, or narrow the implementation so it only handles true project-local configuration files.
